title: Load sharing techniques for electric power sources
abstract: Techniques are described that not only weigh frequency/voltage and real/reactive load sharing separately, but also utilize a window around a setpoint, such as a percentage of an output range, and determine whether there is oscillation. In response, a control system can adjust a time-independent component of a closed-loop controller to weaken the time-independent component to try and reduce the oscillation. The control system can continue to monitor and weaken the time-independent component until it reaches some limit or until it comes back into control.
claims:
Claims 1. A control system (100, 106N, 106B, 106A) to monitor and control a first electric power source (102N, 102B, 102A) coupled in parallel with a second electric power source (102N, 102B, 102A), wherein the first electric power source (102N, 102B, 102A) and the second electric power source (102N, 102B, 102A) are configured to share a load, the control system (100, 106N, 106B, 106A) comprising: a processor (108A) configured to: receive a representation of a first measurement and a representation of a second measurement; generate corresponding error values (305, 303, 205, 203) for the first measurement and the second measurement and combine the corresponding error values (305, 303, 205, 203) as a single first error value; compare the single first error value to a first window, wherein the first window defines upper and lower limits about a first characteristic of the first electric power source (102N, 102B, 102A), wherein the first window is configured to generate a first output (330, 230) signal (322, 222) representing an adjustment to a time-independent component (314, 214) of a closed-loop controller (334, 234, 312, 212, 110A); and generate, using the first output (330, 230) signal (322, 222) and the closed-loop controller (334, 234, 312, 212, 110A) having a time-dependent component (316, 216) and the time-independent component (314, 214), a first control signal (328, 228); and a controller (334, 234) coupled to the first electric power source (102N, 102B, 102A) and configured to receive the first control signal (328, 228) and, in response, adjust the first characteristic of the first electric power source (102N, 102B, 102A). 2. The control system (100, 106N, 106B, 106A) of claim 1, wherein the first measurement includes a frequency measurement (202), the 

second measurement includes a real measurement (204), and the first characteristic is a speed of the first electric power source (102N, 102B, 102A). 3. The control system (100, 106N, 106B, 106A) of claim 1, wherein the first measurement includes a voltage measurement (302), the second measurement includes a reactive power measurement, and the first characteristic is a voltage of the first electric power source (102N, 102B, 102A). 4. The control system (100, 106N, 106B, 106A) of claim 1, wherein the processor (108A) is further configured to: receive a representation of a third measurement and a representation of a fourth measurement; generate corresponding error values (305, 303, 205, 203) for the third measurement and the fourth measurement and combine the corresponding error values (305, 303, 205, 203) as a single second error value; and generate, using the single second error value and the closed-loop controller (334, 234, 312, 212, 110A), a second control signal (328, 228), wherein the controller (334, 234) is further configured to receive the second control signal (328, 228) and, in response, adjust a second characteristic of the first electric power source (102N, 102B, 102A). 5. The control system (100, 106N, 106B, 106A) of claim 4, wherein the first measurement includes a frequency measurement (202), the second measurement includes a real power measurement (204), the third measurement includes a voltage measurement (302), and the fourth measurement includes at least one of a reactive power measurement or a power factor measurement. 6. The control system (100, 106N, 106B, 106A) of claim 1, wherein the processor (108A) is further configured to: 

 apply a first filter 306, 232) to the representation of the first measurement and a second filter (332, 308, 306, 232) to the representation of the second measurement, wherein at least one of the first filter (332, 308, 306, 232) and the second filter (332, 308, 306, 232) includes an exponential weighted moving average filter (332, 308, 306, 232). 7. The control system (100, 106N, 106B, 106A) of claim 1, wherein the processor (108A) is further configured to: compare the single first error value to a second window, wherein the second window defines upper and lower limits about a second characteristic of the first electric power source (102N, 102B, 102A), wherein the second window is configured to generate a second output (330, 230) signal (322, 222) representing an adjustment to the time-dependent component (316, 216) of the closed-loop controller (334, 234, 312, 212, 110A). 8. The control system (100, 106N, 106B, 106A) of claim 1, wherein the time-independent component (314, 214) is a proportional component, wherein the time-dependent component (316, 216) is an integral component. 9. The control system (100, 106N, 106B, 106A) of claim 1, wherein the closed-loop controller (334, 234, 312, 212, 110A) includes a proportional-integral-derivative (PID) controller (334, 234). 10. A computer-implemented method (400) of monitoring and controlling a first electric power source (102N, 102B, 102A) coupled in parallel with a second electric power source (102N, 102B, 102A), wherein the first electric power source (102N, 102B, 102A) and the second electric power source (102N, 102B, 102A) are configured to share a load, the computer-implemented method (400) comprising: 

 receiving a of a first measurement and a representation of a second measurement; generating corresponding error values (305, 303, 205, 203) for the first measurement and the second measurement and combining the corresponding error values (305, 303, 205, 203) as a single first error value; comparing the single first error value to a first window, wherein the first window defines upper and lower limits about a first characteristic of the first electric power source (102N, 102B, 102A); generating a first output (330, 230) signal (322, 222) representing an adjustment to a time-independent component (314, 214) of a closed-loop controller (334, 234, 312, 212, 110A); generating, using the first output (330, 230) signal (322, 222) and the closed-loop controller (334, 234, 312, 212, 110A) having a time-dependent component (316, 216) and the time-independent component (314, 214), a first control signal (328, 228); and adjusting, based on the first control signal (328, 228), the first characteristic of the first electric power source (102N, 102B, 102A). 11. The computer-implemented method (400) of claim 10, wherein the first measurement includes a frequency measurement (202), the second measurement includes a real power measurement (204), and the first characteristic is a speed of the first electric power source (102N, 102B, 102A). 12. The computer-implemented method (400) of claim 10, wherein the first measurement includes a voltage measurement (302), the second measurement includes a reactive power measurement, and the first characteristic is a voltage of the first electric power source (102N, 102B, 102A). 13. The computer-implemented method (400) of claim 10, further comprising: 

 receiving a of a third measurement and a representation of a fourth measurement; generating corresponding error values (305, 303, 205, 203) for the third measurement and the fourth measurement and combine the corresponding error values (305, 303, 205, 203) as a single second error value; generating, using the single second error value and the closed-loop controller (334, 234, 312, 212, 110A), a second control signal (328, 228); and adjusting, based on the second control signal (328, 228), a second characteristic of the first electric power source (102N, 102B, 102A). 14. The computer-implemented method (400) of claim 13, wherein the first measurement includes a frequency measurement (202), the second measurement includes a real power measurement (204), the third measurement includes a voltage measurement (302), and the fourth measurement includes at least one of a reactive power measurement or a power factor measurement. 15. The computer-implemented method (400) of claim 10, further comprising: applying a first filter (332, 308, 306, 232) to the representation of the first measurement and a second filter (332, 308, 306, 232) to the representation of the second measurement, wherein at least one of the first filter (332, 308, 306, 232) and the second filter (332, 308, 306, 232) includes an exponential weighted moving average filter (332, 308, 306, 232).
